name: AMERICAN ELECTRIC POWER CO INC
business: American Electric Power Co Inc (AEP) is a public utility holding company that directly owns all outstanding common stock of public utility subsidiaries providing electric service. AEP's subsidiaries generate, transmit, and distribute electricity to retail and wholesale customers across 11 states. The company also develops and owns transmission assets through its AEP Transmission Holdco segment.
sellsTo: Wholesale customers (other electric utility companies, rural electric cooperatives, municipalities)

exposures.sentence: Much of this demand is driven by interconnecting with and providing power to data centers and other large load customers to serve an increasingly digital economy and to support AI.

A significant portion of AEP's earnings is derived from transmission investments and activities.

I&M owns the Cook Plant, which consists of two nuclear generating units for a rated capacity of 2,296 MWs, or about a tenth of the regulated generating capacity in the AEP System as of December 31, 2025.

Risks from cybersecurity and physical threats to energy infrastructure are increasing.

Changes in regulatory policies and advances in batteries or energy storage, wind turbines and photovoltaic solar cells are reducing costs of new technology to levels that are making them competitive with some central station electricity production and delivery.

catalysts.event: Acquisition of Green Country Power Plant
catalysts.sentence: the main consumption increase driver was related to the Green Country Power Plant, which was acquired by PSO on June 30, 2025.

catalysts.event: Closing of 19.9% noncontrolling interest sale in Midwest Transmission Holdings
Arkansas base rate case order approving 9.65% ROE
catalysts.sentence: The APSC issued an order approving a 9.65% ROE effective February 2026.

catalysts.event: Expiration of UPA for Rockport Plant
catalysts.sentence: The UPA will continue in effect until the debt obligations of AEGCo secured by the Rockport Plant have been satisfied and discharged (currently expected to be December 2028).
